items.front: When are recommended altitudes provided on a surveillance approach?
items.back: Provide them only if the pilot requests them, and advise that recommended altitudes will be given for each mile on final down to MDA or circling MDA.

items.front: What visual-reference reports may be requested on a surveillance approach?
items.back: You may request the runway, approach lights, runway lights, or airport in sight, or for point-in-space operations ask when the pilot can proceed visually to the landing area.

items.front: What extra information is needed when a surveillance approach will end in a circle-to-land maneuver?
items.back: Obtain the aircraft approach category and then issue the applicable circling MDA before authorizing final descent.

items.front: What descent instruction is used on a surveillance approach when no descent restriction exists?
items.back: At the descent point, tell the aircraft to descend to its MDA.

items.front: How often should transmissions occur on surveillance final approach guidance?
items.back: Transmit approximately every 15 seconds while providing course guidance and deviation information.

items.front: When must surveillance approach guidance be discontinued?
items.back: Stop it when requested by the pilot, when safe continuation to the MAP is questionable, or when the aircraft is over the MAP.
